title: What's done well: circuit breaker, HTML escaping, signature parser
description:
Three things in this codebase are genuinely well done and worth preserving:

1. Circuit breaker (delivery.rs lines 22-55): Clean, self-contained, correctly resets on success, trips at 10 consecutive failures within one hour. The state is scoped to the worker, not leaked globally.

2. text_to_html (post.rs lines 34-43): Correctly escapes &, <, > before doing paragraph/line-break substitution, so the escaping doesn't eat the markup. A subtle ordering requirement that is handled right.

3. split_signature_params (signatures.rs lines 128-149): Correctly handles quoted commas in HTTP Signature header values — a real correctness edge case that most naive split(',') implementations get wrong. The implementation is minimal and correct.
close_reason: P2-P3: RateLimiter::new returns Self, remaining items accepted as known tech debt or tracked in hardening epic
